test_cases.description: Verify that the repository contains the expected entry point directories and that all private packages reside under internal/.
preconditions: Repository is checked out at project root
test_cases.expected: Directories cmd/af-hub/ and cmd/afc/ exist; all non-entry-point Go packages reside under internal/

test_cases.description: Verify that `make build` compiles both binaries and places them at bin/af-hub and bin/afc with exit code 0.
preconditions: Go 1.22+ is installed
Repository is checked out at project root
No prior build artifacts in bin/
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; files bin/af-hub and bin/afc both exist and are executable
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_command('make build')
assert result.exit_code == 0
assert file_exists('bin/af-hub') and is_executable('bin/af-hub')
assert file_exists('bin/afc') and is_executable('bin/afc')

test_cases.description: Verify that `make test` runs `go test ./...` across all packages and exits with code 0 when all tests pass.
preconditions: Go 1.22+ is installed
Repository is checked out at project root
All test dependencies are available
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; output contains test results for all packages
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_command('make test')
assert result.exit_code == 0
assert 'ok' in result.stdout or result.stdout contains package test lines

test_cases.description: Verify that `make lint` runs `go vet ./...` across all packages and exits with code 0 when no vet errors are found.
preconditions: Go 1.22+ is installed
Repository is checked out at project root
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; no vet error output
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_command('make lint')
assert result.exit_code == 0
assert result.stderr is empty or contains no vet error lines

test_cases.description: Verify that the afc binary stub compiles and exits cleanly with code 0.
preconditions: make build has been run successfully
bin/afc exists
test_cases.expected: Exit code 0; no panic or runtime error output
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = run_command('bin/afc')
assert result.exit_code == 0
assert 'panic' not in result.stderr

test_cases.description: Verify that the configuration loader reads config.toml and applies the correct defaults for all optional fields.
preconditions: config.toml exists in working directory with no optional fields set
test_cases.input: config.toml with only required fields, no port/bind_address/database_path/logging_level specified
test_cases.expected: Loaded config has port=8080, bind_address='0.0.0.0', database.path='./data/af-hub.db', logging.level='info'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = load_config('config.toml')
assert cfg.server.port == 8080
assert cfg.server.bind_address == '0.0.0.0'
assert cfg.database.path == './data/af-hub.db'
assert cfg.logging.level == 'info'

test_cases.description: Verify that the configuration validator accepts all valid log levels and rejects an out-of-range port.
preconditions: config.toml template available
test_cases.expected: Validation returns a non-nil error identifying the invalid field(s)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = {server: {port: 70000}, logging: {level: 'verbose'}}
err = validate_config(cfg)
assert err != nil
assert 'port' in err.message or 'level' in err.message

test_cases.description: Verify that the server creates the database parent directory automatically when it does not exist.
preconditions: config.toml specifies database.path pointing to a non-existent directory
No prior database directory exists
test_cases.expected: Directory './data/nonexistent/' is created before the database file is opened; no error returned
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
remove_dir_if_exists('./data/nonexistent')
cfg.database.path = './data/nonexistent/af-hub.db'
initialize_database(cfg)
assert dir_exists('./data/nonexistent/')

test_cases.description: Verify that when external_url is set in config.toml, the value is loaded and accessible to other subsystems.
preconditions: config.toml contains [server] external_url = 'https://example.com'
test_cases.expected: Loaded config has external_url='https://example.com'; no validation error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
cfg = load_config('config.toml')
assert cfg.server.external_url == 'https://example.com'
assert validate_config(cfg) == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that WAL mode is enabled immediately after opening the SQLite database by querying PRAGMA journal_mode.
preconditions: Valid config.toml exists
Database file does not yet exist
test_cases.input: call database open and initialization routine
test_cases.expected: PRAGMA journal_mode returns 'wal' after initialization
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db = open_database(cfg.database.path)
row = db.query_row('PRAGMA journal_mode')
assert row.value == 'wal'

test_cases.description: Verify that all five tables are created by the schema initialization routine.
preconditions: SQLite database is opened with WAL mode enabled
test_cases.input: run schema initialization (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements)
test_cases.expected: Tables users, workspaces, workspace_members, api_keys, and admin_tokens all exist in the database
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
initialize_schema(db)
for table_name in ['users', 'workspaces', 'workspace_members', 'api_keys', 'admin_tokens']:
  rows = db.query("SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?", table_name)
  assert len(rows) == 1

test_cases.description: Verify that the UNIQUE constraint on (provider, provider_id) in users, composite PK in workspace_members, and UNIQUE constraints in workspaces are enforced by the database schema.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with all tables
test_cases.input: attempt to insert duplicate (provider, provider_id) into users; attempt duplicate (user_id, workspace_id) into workspace_members; attempt duplicate name/slug/url into workspaces
test_cases.expected: Each duplicate insert returns a UNIQUE constraint violation error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
insert_user(provider='local', provider_id='admin')
err = insert_user(provider='local', provider_id='admin')
assert err is UNIQUE_CONSTRAINT_ERROR

insert_workspace_member(user_id='u1', workspace_id='w1')
err = insert_workspace_member(user_id='u1', workspace_id='w1')
assert err is UNIQUE_CONSTRAINT_ERROR

test_cases.description: Verify that timestamps and primary keys are stored as RFC 3339 strings and UUID TEXT values respectively.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with all tables
Store layer is used to create a user
test_cases.input: create a user via the store layer
test_cases.expected: created_at and updated_at columns contain valid RFC 3339 strings; id column contains a valid UUID string
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
user = store.create_user({username: 'test', provider: 'local', provider_id: 'test1'})
row = db.query_row('SELECT id, created_at, updated_at FROM users WHERE id=?', user.id)
assert parse_uuid(row.id) is valid
assert time.parse(RFC3339, row.created_at) is not error
assert time.parse(RFC3339, row.updated_at) is not error

test_cases.description: Verify that the store layer provides typed CRUD functions for all five entities and returns a sentinel not-found error for missing records.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with all five tables
test_cases.input: call get_user_by_id with a non-existent ID
test_cases.expected: Function returns (nil, ErrNotFound) sentinel error; similar pattern applies for all five entities
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
user, err = store.get_user_by_id('nonexistent-uuid')
assert user == nil
assert err == ErrNotFound

ws, err = store.get_workspace_by_id('nonexistent-uuid')
assert ws == nil
assert err == ErrNotFound

test_cases.description: Verify that the store layer generates UUIDs for new entity IDs and formats timestamps as RFC 3339 strings.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with all five tables
test_cases.input: call store.create_user with valid input (no id or timestamps provided by caller)
test_cases.expected: Returned user has a non-empty UUID id and created_at/updated_at values parseable as RFC 3339
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
user, err = store.create_user({username: 'bob', provider: 'local', provider_id: 'bob1'})
assert err == nil
assert is_valid_uuid(user.id)
assert time.parse(RFC3339, user.created_at) is not error

test_cases.description: Verify that no package outside the store layer package imports database/sql or executes SQL statements directly.
preconditions: Repository is checked out at project root
test_cases.input: static analysis of all Go source files outside internal/store/ (or equivalent store package path)
test_cases.expected: No Go file outside the store package contains a direct call to db.Query, db.Exec, or db.QueryRow
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
files = find_go_files(exclude_path='internal/store')
for f in files:
  assert 'db.Query(' not in f.content
  assert 'db.Exec(' not in f.content
  assert 'db.QueryRow(' not in f.content
  assert import of 'database/sql' is not present (except via store layer)

test_cases.description: Verify that the store layer wraps database errors with contextual information before returning to callers.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized
A simulated DB error is injected (e.g. via a mock or closed DB)
test_cases.input: call store.create_user on a closed database connection
test_cases.expected: Returned error is non-nil, contains operation name and entity type in error message, and does not panic
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.close()
user, err = store.create_user({username: 'x', provider: 'local', provider_id: 'x1'})
assert err != nil
assert 'create' in err.message or 'user' in err.message
assert no panic occurred

test_cases.description: Verify that on first boot (zero users in DB), the admin bootstrap routine creates the admin user with the expected field values.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with all tables and zero rows in users table
test_cases.expected: A user record exists in users table with username='admin', email='admin@localhost', provider='local', provider_id='admin', status='active'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
run_admin_bootstrap(db, cfg)
user = db.query_row('SELECT * FROM users WHERE username=?', 'admin')
assert user.username == 'admin'
assert user.email == 'admin@localhost'
assert user.provider == 'local'
assert user.provider_id == 'admin'
assert user.status == 'active'

test_cases.description: Verify that the admin token generation produces a token matching the format 'af_admin_<64 hex chars>'.
preconditions: crypto/rand is available
test_cases.expected: Token string starts with 'af_admin_' and has exactly 73 characters total (9 prefix + 64 hex)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
token, err = generate_admin_token()
assert err == nil
assert token starts_with('af_admin_')
assert len(token) == 73
assert is_hex_string(token[9:])
assert len(token[9:]) == 64

test_cases.description: Verify that the SHA-256 hash of the generated admin token is stored in the admin_tokens table.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with all tables
Admin user has been created
test_cases.input: run token hash persistence step of admin bootstrap
test_cases.expected: admin_tokens table has one row; token_hash equals sha256(plaintext_token) in hex
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
token, _ = generate_admin_token()
store_admin_token_hash(db, token)
row = db.query_row('SELECT token_hash FROM admin_tokens LIMIT 1')
expected_hash = hex(sha256(token))
assert row.token_hash == expected_hash

test_cases.description: Verify that the admin_token file is written next to config.toml with file permissions 0600 and contains the plaintext token.
preconditions: config.toml exists in working directory
Admin bootstrap is in progress
test_cases.expected: File 'admin_token' exists in config directory, has mode 0600, and its content equals the plaintext token
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
token = 'af_admin_' + 'a' * 64
write_admin_token_file(token, config_dir)
f = stat('config_dir/admin_token')
assert f.mode == 0600
assert read_file('config_dir/admin_token') == token

test_cases.description: Verify that the absolute path of the admin_token file is logged at the warn level after the file is written.
preconditions: logrus is configured with a test log capture
admin_token file has been written
test_cases.input: run the admin bootstrap log step
test_cases.expected: A warn-level log entry is emitted containing the absolute path to the admin_token file
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
log_entries = capture_logs(level='warn')
run_admin_bootstrap_log_step(config_dir)
assert any(entry.level == 'warn' and abs_path(config_dir+'/admin_token') in entry.message for entry in log_entries)

test_cases.description: Verify that on a non-first boot the server reads AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN, hashes it, and succeeds when the hash matches the stored token_hash.
preconditions: SQLite database initialized with at least one user
admin_tokens table has a stored token_hash matching the test token's SHA-256
test_cases.input: AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN env var set to the correct plaintext token
test_cases.expected: validate_admin_token returns nil error; server proceeds past validation
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
set_env('AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN', correct_token)
err = validate_admin_token(db)
assert err == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that on a non-first boot with AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN absent or empty, the server logs a fatal error and refuses to start.
preconditions: SQLite database initialized with at least one user
admin_tokens table has a stored token_hash
test_cases.expected: validate_admin_token returns a non-nil error mentioning the missing variable; server exits with non-zero before binding a port
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
unset_env('AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN')
err = validate_admin_token(db)
assert err != nil
assert 'AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN' in err.message or 'missing' in err.message

test_cases.description: Verify that on a non-first boot with a wrong AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN, the server logs a fatal error indicating token mismatch and refuses to start.
preconditions: SQLite database initialized with at least one user
admin_tokens table has a stored token_hash
test_cases.input: AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN set to an incorrect value
test_cases.expected: validate_admin_token returns a non-nil error mentioning token mismatch; server exits with non-zero before binding a port
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
set_env('AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN', 'af_admin_' + 'b' * 64)
err = validate_admin_token(db)
assert err != nil
assert 'mismatch' in err.message or 'invalid' in err.message

test_cases.description: Verify that --reset-admin-token generates a new token, updates admin_tokens, overwrites the admin_token file with mode 0600, logs the file path at warn level, and continues normal startup.
preconditions: SQLite database initialized with at least one user and an existing admin_tokens record
admin_token file exists from previous boot
test_cases.expected: New token_hash in admin_tokens differs from old hash; admin_token file contains new plaintext token with mode 0600; warn log entry contains file path; server starts normally
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
old_hash = db.query_row('SELECT token_hash FROM admin_tokens LIMIT 1').token_hash
run_token_rotation(db, config_dir)
new_hash = db.query_row('SELECT token_hash FROM admin_tokens LIMIT 1').token_hash
assert new_hash != old_hash
new_token = read_file(config_dir + '/admin_token')
assert new_token starts_with('af_admin_')
assert file_mode(config_dir + '/admin_token') == 0600
assert warn_log_contains(abs_path(config_dir + '/admin_token'))

test_cases.description: Verify that when --reset-admin-token is provided, the AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN environment validation step is skipped.
preconditions: SQLite database initialized with at least one user
admin_tokens table has existing record
--reset-admin-token flag is set
test_cases.input: AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN is unset; --reset-admin-token flag is provided
test_cases.expected: Server proceeds past the admin token validation step without error despite missing AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
unset_env('AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN')
err = run_startup_with_reset_flag(db, cfg)
assert err == nil or err does not relate to missing AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /healthz returns HTTP 200 with JSON body {"status": "ok"} unconditionally.
preconditions: Server is running and bound to configured port
All startup validation has passed
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200 response, Content-Type: application/json, body: {"status": "ok"}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_get('http://localhost:8080/healthz')
assert resp.status_code == 200
assert resp.headers['Content-Type'] contains 'application/json'
assert parse_json(resp.body) == {status: 'ok'}

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /readyz returns HTTP 200 with JSON body {"status": "ready"} when the database ping succeeds.
preconditions: Server is running with a healthy database connection
test_cases.expected: HTTP 200, Content-Type: application/json, body: {"status": "ready"}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = http_get('http://localhost:8080/readyz')
assert resp.status_code == 200
assert resp.headers['Content-Type'] contains 'application/json'
assert parse_json(resp.body) == {status: 'ready'}

test_cases.description: Verify that GET /readyz returns HTTP 503 with JSON body {"status": "not ready"} when the database ping fails.
preconditions: Server is running
Database connection is forced to fail (e.g. file removed or connection closed)
test_cases.input: HTTP GET request to /readyz with broken DB
test_cases.expected: HTTP 503, Content-Type: application/json, body: {"status": "not ready"}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
simulate_db_failure()
resp = http_get('http://localhost:8080/readyz')
assert resp.status_code == 503
assert parse_json(resp.body) == {status: 'not ready'}

test_cases.description: Verify that logrus is configured with JSONFormatter as the sole logging backend.
preconditions: Server logging setup routine has been called
test_cases.expected: logrus global formatter is set to JSONFormatter; log output for a test message is valid JSON
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
configure_logging('info')
log_output = capture_log_output()
logrus.info('test message')
assert parse_json(log_output) is valid JSON
assert log_output contains 'msg' and 'level' fields

test_cases.description: Verify that the request logger middleware emits a structured JSON log entry with method, path, status code, and duration after each HTTP request.
preconditions: Server is running with request logger middleware registered
logrus output is captured
test_cases.expected: A JSON log line is emitted containing fields: method='GET', path='/healthz', status=200, and a numeric duration in milliseconds
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
log_entries = capture_log_output()
http_get('http://localhost:8080/healthz')
entry = parse_json(last_log_entry(log_entries))
assert entry.method == 'GET'
assert entry.path == '/healthz'
assert entry.status == 200
assert entry.duration is numeric

test_cases.description: Verify that the server applies the configured log level from config.toml to the logrus global log level before starting the HTTP server.
preconditions: config.toml specifies logging.level='debug'
test_cases.expected: logrus.GetLevel() returns logrus.DebugLevel
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
configure_logging('debug')
assert logrus.get_level() == DEBUG_LEVEL

configure_logging('warn')
assert logrus.get_level() == WARN_LEVEL

test_cases.description: Verify that the server stops accepting new connections and begins graceful shutdown with a 15-second drain timeout when SIGTERM is received.
preconditions: Server is running and accepting connections
test_cases.input: send SIGTERM to the running af-hub process
test_cases.expected: Server initiates Echo.Shutdown with a context deadline of 15 seconds; new connection attempts are refused after signal is received
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
server_pid = start_server()
send_signal(server_pid, SIGTERM)
assert server_stops_accepting_connections()
assert shutdown_initiated_with_timeout(15 * seconds)

test_cases.description: Verify that the server closes the database connection and exits with code 0 when all in-flight connections drain within the 15-second timeout.
preconditions: Server is running
No long-lived in-flight requests
test_cases.input: send SIGTERM to the running af-hub process
test_cases.expected: Process exits with code 0; database connection is closed before exit
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
server_pid = start_server()
send_signal(server_pid, SIGTERM)
exit_code = wait_for_process(server_pid, timeout=20)
assert exit_code == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that the server force-closes connections, logs a warning, and exits after the 15-second drain timeout expires.
preconditions: Server is running
A long-lived in-flight request that will not complete within 15 seconds is active
test_cases.input: send SIGTERM; in-flight request is held open beyond 15 seconds
test_cases.expected: Server force-closes connections after ~15 seconds, logs a forced-shutdown warning, and exits with non-zero code
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
start_long_running_request()
send_signal(server_pid, SIGTERM)
exit_code = wait_for_process(server_pid, timeout=20)
assert exit_code != 0
assert warn_log_contains('forced') or warn_log_contains('timeout')

test_cases.description: Verify that README.md exists at the project root and contains the required sections.
preconditions: Repository is checked out at project root
test_cases.expected: File exists; contains sections for project overview, prerequisites (Go 1.22+), quickstart (build/configure/run/first boot), project structure, and links to docs/architecture.md and docs/configuration.md
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = read_file('README.md')
assert 'Go 1.22' in content
assert 'make build' in content
assert 'docs/architecture.md' in content
assert 'docs/configuration.md' in content

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/architecture.md exists and covers the required architecture topics.
preconditions: Repository is checked out at project root
test_cases.expected: File exists; covers two-binary design, internal/ package layout, SQLite storage, config loading flow, and request lifecycle
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = read_file('docs/architecture.md')
assert 'af-hub' in content
assert 'afc' in content
assert 'internal' in content
assert 'SQLite' in content
assert 'config' in content

test_cases.description: Verify that docs/configuration.md exists and documents all config.toml fields with types, defaults, and validation rules.
preconditions: Repository is checked out at project root
test_cases.expected: File exists; documents port, bind_address, external_url, database.path, logging.level, and auth.oauth fields with their types, defaults, and validation rules
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = read_file('docs/configuration.md')
assert 'port' in content
assert 'bind_address' in content
assert 'database' in content
assert 'logging' in content
assert 'AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN' in content

property_tests.description: For any generated admin token, the SHA-256 hash stored in admin_tokens always equals sha256(plaintext_token).
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate 100 random admin tokens using generate_admin_token(); for each, store the hash and read it back
property_tests.invariant_check: assert hex(sha256(token)) == stored_token_hash for every generated token

property_tests.description: Admin bootstrap executes if and only if zero users exist in the users table; it never executes more than once for the same first-boot event.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For any startup sequence where users table has zero rows: run bootstrap exactly once. For any sequence where users table has >= 1 row: bootstrap is not triggered.
property_tests.invariant_check: after run_admin_bootstrap(db): assert db.query_count('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users') == 1 (only admin created); call run_admin_bootstrap(db) again: assert db.query_count('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users') == 1 (idempotent, no second admin); for db with 1+ users: assert bootstrap_is_skipped()

property_tests.description: For any SQL statement executed, it originates exclusively from within the store layer package.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Static analysis pass over all Go source files; for any file outside internal/store/, assert no direct SQL execution
property_tests.invariant_check: for each .go file not in internal/store/: assert 'db.Query(' not in content AND 'db.Exec(' not in content AND 'db.QueryRow(' not in content

property_tests.description: For any timestamp value written to any table column by the store layer, the value is a valid RFC 3339 string parseable by time.Parse.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Create one record in each of users, workspaces, workspace_members, api_keys, admin_tokens; for each, read back all timestamp columns
property_tests.invariant_check: for each timestamp_value in all_table_timestamps: assert time.parse(RFC3339, timestamp_value) does not error

property_tests.description: For any server startup sequence that fails validation (config, schema, or token), the server never binds to a TCP port before all validations pass.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For each failure scenario (missing config, invalid port, wrong admin token, missing admin token): run server startup and concurrently poll the configured port
property_tests.invariant_check: for each failure_scenario in [missing_config, invalid_port, bad_admin_token, empty_admin_token]: start_server_async(); poll_port(8080, for_duration=5s); assert port_never_bound(); assert process_exited_non_zero()

property_tests.description: For any database open sequence, PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL is always executed before any CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statement.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Instrument db.Exec to record the order of SQL statements; run database initialization 10 times with fresh databases
property_tests.invariant_check:
sql_log = record_sql_execution_order(initialize_database)
wal_index = index_of('PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL', sql_log)
for each create_table_stmt in sql_log where 'CREATE TABLE' in stmt:
  assert wal_index < index_of(create_table_stmt, sql_log)

property_tests.description: The schema initialization loop over five tables always terminates — it never executes more CREATE TABLE statements than the fixed set of five tables.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Run initialize_schema on any valid SQLite database; count the number of CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements executed
property_tests.invariant_check:
sql_log = record_sql_execution_order(initialize_schema)
create_table_calls = [s for s in sql_log if 'CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS' in s]
assert len(create_table_calls) == 5

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that make build emits compiler error to stderr and exits with non-zero when a source file has a compilation error, without producing partial binaries.
preconditions: A source file in cmd/af-hub/ is temporarily modified to contain a syntax error
bin/ directory is clean
edge_case_tests.input: run `make build` with a deliberately broken source file
edge_case_tests.expected: Exit code non-zero; stderr contains compiler error output; bin/af-hub and bin/afc do not exist or were not overwritten
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
inject_syntax_error('cmd/af-hub/main.go')
result = run_command('make build')
assert result.exit_code != 0
assert len(result.stderr) > 0
assert not file_exists('bin/af-hub') or file_unchanged('bin/af-hub')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the server logs a fatal error and exits with non-zero code when config.toml is absent, without binding any port.
preconditions: No config.toml exists in the working directory
No server is already bound to the configured port
edge_case_tests.input: invoke bin/af-hub in a directory without config.toml
edge_case_tests.expected: Process exits with non-zero code; no port is bound; stderr/log contains fatal error mentioning config.toml
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
ensure_no_file('config.toml')
result = run_server_process()
assert result.exit_code != 0
assert 'config.toml' in result.stderr or 'fatal' in result.log_output
assert port_not_bound(8080)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the server logs a descriptive fatal error identifying the invalid field when config.toml contains an out-of-range port, and exits before binding.
preconditions: config.toml exists with port = 99999
edge_case_tests.expected: validate_config returns non-nil error mentioning 'port'; server exits non-zero without binding
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
write_config_file({server: {port: 99999}})
err = validate_config(load_config('config.toml'))
assert err != nil
assert 'port' in err.message

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the server logs a fatal error with the OS error and exits non-zero when the data directory cannot be created due to a permission error.
preconditions: config.toml specifies database.path under a directory where the process lacks write permission
edge_case_tests.expected: Server exits with non-zero code; log contains OS permission error
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
set_database_path('/root/protected/af-hub.db')
result = run_server_process()
assert result.exit_code != 0
assert 'permission' in result.log_output or 'mkdir' in result.log_output

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the server logs a fatal error and exits non-zero when the SQLite database file cannot be opened, without leaving an open connection.
preconditions: Database directory exists but is not writable by the process
edge_case_tests.input: attempt to open database in a read-only directory
edge_case_tests.expected: open_database returns a non-nil error; server exits with non-zero code; no database file descriptor is left open
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
make_dir_read_only(db_dir)
db, err = open_database(db_dir + '/af-hub.db')
assert err != nil
assert db == nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the server logs a fatal error identifying the failing table and closes the DB connection when a CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statement fails.
preconditions: Database is open
A table named 'users' already exists with an incompatible schema (conflicting column type)
edge_case_tests.input: run initialize_schema(db) on a database with a conflicting 'users' table
edge_case_tests.expected: initialize_schema returns a non-nil error mentioning the table name; database connection is closed
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
db.exec('CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY)')
err = initialize_schema(db)
assert err != nil
assert 'users' in err.message or is db connection closed

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the store layer returns a typed constraint-violation error when a create operation violates a UNIQUE constraint.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized
A user with username='duplicate' already exists
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns non-nil error of a constraint-violation type; no panic
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
store.create_user({username: 'duplicate', provider: 'local', provider_id: 'dup1'})
_, err = store.create_user({username: 'duplicate', provider: 'local', provider_id: 'dup2'})
assert err != nil
assert is_constraint_violation_error(err)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the store layer returns a wrapped error with operation context and does not call os.Exit when the database connection is lost mid-query.
preconditions: SQLite database is initialized
Database connection is closed/lost mid-operation
edge_case_tests.input: close database connection then call store.get_user_by_id
edge_case_tests.expected: Returns non-nil wrapped error containing operation context; does not panic; does not call os.Exit
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
db.close()
_, err = store.get_user_by_id('some-uuid')
assert err != nil
assert 'get' in err.message or 'user' in err.message
assert no panic occurred
assert os_exit_not_called()

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when the admin_token file cannot be written during bootstrap, the server removes any partial file, removes the admin user record, logs fatal, and exits non-zero.
preconditions: SQLite database initialized with zero users
Config directory is read-only so admin_token file cannot be written
edge_case_tests.input: run admin bootstrap with write-protected config directory
edge_case_tests.expected: Server exits with non-zero code; no admin_token file exists; users table is empty after cleanup; fatal log entry emitted
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
make_dir_read_only(config_dir)
result = run_admin_bootstrap(db, config_dir)
assert result.exit_code != 0
assert not file_exists(config_dir + '/admin_token')
assert db.query_count('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users') == 0

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when crypto/rand fails to generate random bytes, the server propagates the error and does not leave partial state on disk.
preconditions: crypto/rand is mocked to return an error
edge_case_tests.input: call generate_admin_token() with rand.Read returning an error
edge_case_tests.expected: generate_admin_token returns non-nil error; no admin_token file written; no partial entry in admin_tokens table
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mock_rand_read_to_fail()
token, err = generate_admin_token()
assert err != nil
assert token == ''
assert not file_exists(config_dir + '/admin_token')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the server logs a fatal error about corrupted token state and exits non-zero when admin_tokens table is empty on a non-first boot.
preconditions: SQLite database initialized with at least one user in users table
admin_tokens table is empty
edge_case_tests.input: AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN is set to a valid-looking token; run admin token validation
edge_case_tests.expected: validate_admin_token returns non-nil error mentioning empty/corrupted token state; server exits with non-zero
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
set_env('AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN', 'af_admin_' + 'c' * 64)
ensure_admin_tokens_table_empty(db)
err = validate_admin_token(db)
assert err != nil
assert 'corrupt' in err.message or 'empty' in err.message or 'no token' in err.message

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that when the admin_token file cannot be overwritten during token rotation, the server does not update admin_tokens, logs fatal, leaves old token intact, and exits non-zero.
preconditions: SQLite database initialized with at least one user
admin_tokens has an existing record
admin_token file exists but config directory is made read-only
edge_case_tests.input: run token rotation with --reset-admin-token flag; admin_token file cannot be overwritten
edge_case_tests.expected: admin_tokens token_hash is unchanged from before rotation attempt; server exits with non-zero; old admin_token file content is preserved
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
old_hash = db.query_row('SELECT token_hash FROM admin_tokens LIMIT 1').token_hash
old_token_content = read_file(config_dir + '/admin_token')
make_dir_read_only(config_dir)
err = run_token_rotation(db, config_dir)
assert err != nil
new_hash = db.query_row('SELECT token_hash FROM admin_tokens LIMIT 1').token_hash
assert new_hash == old_hash

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that GET /readyz returns HTTP 503 with body {"status": "not ready"} and a warn log when the database ping hangs beyond the 2-second deadline.
preconditions: Server is running
Database ping is mocked to block indefinitely
edge_case_tests.input: HTTP GET request to /readyz with a hung DB ping mock
edge_case_tests.expected: Response arrives within approximately 2 seconds with HTTP 503 and body {"status": "not ready"}; warn-level log entry emitted mentioning timeout
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mock_db_ping_to_block()
start = now()
resp = http_get('http://localhost:8080/readyz')
elapsed = now() - start
assert elapsed < 3 * seconds
assert resp.status_code == 503
assert parse_json(resp.body) == {status: 'not ready'}
assert warn_log_contains('timeout') or warn_log_contains('deadline')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the server applies a 5-second timeout to the database close operation and exits without blocking indefinitely when the close call hangs.
preconditions: Server graceful shutdown has completed drain phase
Database close call is mocked to block indefinitely
edge_case_tests.input: trigger shutdown drain completion with a mocked blocking db.Close()
edge_case_tests.expected: Server exits within approximately 5 seconds of the drain completing; logs an error about the timeout; exits without blocking indefinitely
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
mock_db_close_to_block()
send_signal(server_pid, SIGTERM)
start = now()
wait_for_process(server_pid, timeout=30)
elapsed = now() - start
assert elapsed < 10 * seconds
assert log_contains('close') or log_contains('timeout')

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test the full first-boot path: server starts with config.toml and no AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN, bootstraps admin, writes admin_token file, and serves health probes.
smoke_tests.trigger: Invoke bin/af-hub with a valid config.toml in the working directory and no AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable set
real_components: admin_token file written to config directory
expected_effects: Server process starts and remains running
admin_token file is created next to config.toml with mode 0600 and content matching 'af_admin_<64 hex chars>'
admin_tokens table contains one row with token_hash = sha256(plaintext_token)
users table contains one row with username='admin'
GET /healthz returns HTTP 200 with body {"status": "ok"}
GET /readyz returns HTTP 200 with body {"status": "ready"}
Warn-level log entry contains the absolute path to the admin_token file

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test the subsequent boot path: server starts with AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN set to the correct token and passes validation before serving traffic.
smoke_tests.trigger: Invoke bin/af-hub with AF_HUB_ADMIN_TOKEN set to the plaintext token from a prior first-boot admin_token file
real_components: SQLite database from prior first boot
expected_effects: Server process starts and remains running without fatal error
Admin token validation passes silently
GET /healthz returns HTTP 200 with body {"status": "ok"}
GET /readyz returns HTTP 200 with body {"status": "ready"}
No new admin_token file is written

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test the admin token rotation path: server invoked with --reset-admin-token generates a new token, updates the DB, overwrites the admin_token file, and starts normally.
smoke_tests.trigger: Invoke bin/af-hub --reset-admin-token with a database from a prior boot and an existing admin_token file
real_components: SQLite database from prior boot with admin_tokens record
expected_effects: admin_token file content is different from the old token
admin_tokens table token_hash differs from the old hash
New admin_token file has mode 0600
Warn-level log entry contains absolute path to admin_token file
Server starts normally and GET /healthz returns HTTP 200

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test the graceful shutdown path: running server drains in-flight requests and exits cleanly on SIGTERM.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send SIGTERM to a running af-hub process with no active in-flight requests
expected_effects: Server stops accepting new connections after SIGTERM
Server process exits with code 0 within 16 seconds
No error or panic in log output related to shutdown

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test the readiness probe failure path: GET /readyz returns HTTP 503 when database ping fails.
smoke_tests.trigger: Send GET /readyz to a running server while the database connection is forced to fail
mockable: SQLite database ping (forced to return error or block)
expected_effects: HTTP 503 response received with body {"status": "not ready"}
Response arrives within 3 seconds (respecting 2-second deadline)
Warn-level log entry emitted mentioning the ping failure or timeout
